mnemonics: WEIGHTED NEIGHBORHOOD: Weighting points by proximity to make local predictions, unlike a 'One-Size-Fits-All' global model.
keyPoints: LWR is non-parametric, meaning it doesn't assume a fixed global form for the function f.
It makes a prediction for a query point x by fitting a linear model locally, weighted by the proximity of nearby training points.
A kernel function determines the weights, assigning higher importance to points near x and lower importance to points farther away.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that LWR requires the entire training dataset to be stored and accessed to make each new prediction, making it computationally expensive.
Choosing an inappropriate bandwidth parameter for the kernel (tau); too small leads to overfitting, too large leads to underfitting and loses the 'local' aspect.
